Date: June 16, 2026

1. Tribal Development Department Government Decision No. ASHA 1099 / Q. No. 2/99 Ka. 11, D. 01.02.1999.
Tribal Development Department Government Decision No. Shaasha 2012 / Q. 370 / Ka. 13, D. 23.01.2014.

7. Tribal Development Department Circular No. Aasha-2026 / P.No.93 / Ka. 11, d. 07.05.2026.
The Proposal: -
To promote education among tribals in the hilly and remote areas of the state of Maharashtra.
The government started the "Ashramshala Group" scheme from the year 1972-73.
There are a total of 556 aided ashramshalas run by NGOs out of which 37 are
Primary Aided Ashramshalas (Classes 1 to 7) and 319 Secondary Aided Ashramshalas
(Classes 1 to 10) are in progress. As well as 200 junior colleges attached to secondary ashramshalas.
(Classes 1 to 12) are in progress. 5 subsidized elementary from this academic year 2026-27
Upgradation of Ashramshalas from 8th to 10th standard and 19 Aided Secondary Ashramshalas.
Affiliated Junior Colleges (Classes 11 and 12, Arts and Science stream)
It was under the consideration of the Government.
Cabinet approves grant-in-aid to 5 NGOs operating in the State under Tribal Development Department
Department of Upgradation of Primary Ashramshalas and 19 Aided Secondary Ashramshalas
The proposal has been approved. Accordingly, the Government is now taking the following decisions in this regard.
The Government's Decision-
Pursuant to the approval given by the Hon'ble Cabinet in its meeting dated 05.05.2026
Projects under Additional Commissioner, Tribal Development, Nagpur under Tribal Development Department
Officer, Integrated Tribal Development Project, Chandrapur Subordinate Public Welfare Backward Classes Education
Institution, Karanji, Ta. Gondpimpri, G. Chandrapur Operated Aided Ashramshala, Gojoli, Ta.
Secondary Ashram School Gondpimpri, G. Chandrapur upgraded from academic year 2026-27
The institute is being approved to start classes for 11th and 12th (Arts and Science).
The following terms and conditions will apply.
1. Public Welfare Backward Class Educational Institutions, Karanji Ta. Gondpimpri G. Chandrapur Institute
Graduation classes to begin immediately after receiving orders regarding Ashram Shrine
Commissioner, Tribal Development, Nashik & Upper within 30 days of commencement of classes.
Commissioner, Tribal Development, Amravati as well as Project Officer, Integrated Tribal Development
The project should be reported to Chandrapur.
Academic Year 2026-27 to Class 11 (Arts & Science), Academic Year
Classes for Class 12 (Arts and Science) should be started from 2027-28.
3. "Ashramshala" for Aided Ashramshala to the concerned upgraded institution.
Equipped with all physical facilities as per the standards mentioned in the Code-2019.
The Ashramshala and Hostel Institute has its own well-equipped concrete building.
It will be mandatory to build within two years from the date. The agency has not acted on the matter in 2 years.
If done, the grant-in-aid ashramshala will be permanently de-recognised.
4. The institution shall arrange for classrooms and accommodation in the Ashramshala in a separate building. as well as
The building should be provided with standard bathrooms, toilets, and clean water.
Ample arrangements should also be made available.
5. To raise the standard of education in the aided ashrams where grading has been approved.
It's necessary. In view of this, the annual result of the students of such ashramshalas is at least 80.
There should be a percentage and at least 60 per cent of them should get first class.
6. Orders for recruitment of posts to be made available by way of promotion under the Finance Department.
They will be issued separately after approval by the High Powered Committee.
7. Public Welfare Backward Class Educational Institutions, Karanji, Ta. Gondpimpri, G. Chandrapur Operated
Aided Ashramshala, Gojoli, Ta. Government at the Ashram in Gondpimpri, G. Chandrapur
Subsidies will be admissible as per the formula prescribed from time to time.
8. 'Ashramshala Samhita' for Ashramshala funded by the Institute as well as by the Government from time to time.
Comply with the terms, conditions, and regulations set forth.
9. All the resident students of the Ashramshala shall be from the Scheduled Tribes.
10. If it is found that the conditions, conditions and rules prescribed by the Government are not complied with.
Also, if there are complaints of malpractice / mismanagement regarding the aforesaid Ashramshala
The accreditation of the institute's ashramshala will be cancelled.
11. Right of Children to Compulsory and Free Education Act, 2009.
To provide facilities to the students and to maintain the standard of education.
will be binding on the organization.
12. The Institute runs all the Ashramshalas under the Tribal Development Department in the state.
Strict implementation of safety measures for students / students.
In this regard, No. 4 in the Government Decision dated 09.09.2024 herein.
The provisions will have to be strictly adhered to.
13. Reference No. 7 here. Tribal Development in Government Circular dated 07.05.2026
Aided Ashramshalas run by NGOs under the department.
Guidelines regarding admission of students should be strictly followed.
14. Commissioner, Tribal Development, Nashik to take necessary action in the aforesaid matter.
It should be done immediately. The expenditure for this is the corresponding budget of the subsidized ashramshalas.
Expenditure should be deducted under the head of accounts and should be met from the sanctioned grants.
(2) The decision of the Government. The Cabinet in its meeting dated 05.05.2026
It is being issued under approval.
